articles.headline: Centre Launches National Urban Climate Resilience Mission 2.0 to Bolster City Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet on Monday approved the launch of the National Urban Climate Resilience Mission (NUCRM) 2.0, a significant upgrade to the existing framework.
• The mission aims to enhance urban infrastructure resilience against climate change impacts, focusing on water security, waste management, and green infrastructure.
• It will promote climate-responsive urban planning, early warning systems, and capacity building for municipal bodies and local communities.
• A substantial allocation has been made for integrated urban water management projects, including rainwater harvesting and wastewater recycling plants.
• The mission emphasizes public-private partnerships and the adoption of nature-based solutions for sustainable urban development.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: NUCRM 2.0 launched by Union Cabinet.
Focus areas: water security, waste management, green infrastructure.
Aims to enhance urban infrastructure resilience.
Promotes public-private partnerships and nature-based solutions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of NUCRM 2.0 in achieving India's climate commitments and SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities).
Analyze the challenges in urban climate resilience and how NUCRM 2.0 proposes to address them.
Evaluate the role of integrated urban water management and green infrastructure in mitigating climate risks in Indian cities.
Examine the potential for public-private partnerships and community involvement in the success of such missions.
articles.imageDescription: Conceptual image of a green, resilient smart city with sustainable infrastructure.
articles.trivia: Globally, over half of humanity lives in urban areas, and this proportion is expected to reach 68% by 2050, making urban resilience critical for future sustainability.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Robust Digital Privacy Framework, Stresses Data Protection Compliance
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today delivered a landmark judgment, upholding the core tenets of the recently enacted Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA).
• The ruling emphasized the fundamental right to privacy in the digital age, mandating stringent compliance for data fiduciaries and processors.
• It clarified certain ambiguities regarding cross-border data transfers and the 'significant data fiduciary' classification.
• The court also directed the government to expedite the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India.
• This judgment is expected to significantly impact how technology companies operate and handle user data in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA).
Emphasized fundamental right to privacy.
Clarified cross-border data transfer rules.
Directed establishment of Data Protection Board of India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on digital privacy for individuals and corporations in India.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities presented by the DPDPA in fostering a secure digital economy.
Examine the balance between national security concerns and individual privacy rights in the context of digital data protection.
Evaluate India's position in global data governance with the enforcement of its robust data protection law.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel resting on a legal document with a digital lock icon, symbolizing data protection.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, paving the way for data protection legislation.

articles.headline: India-EU High-Level Trade Dialogue Sees Significant Progress on Key Sectors
articles.explainer:
• The 14th round of the India-EU High-Level Dialogue on Trade and Investment concluded with positive outcomes, signaling potential breakthroughs in FTA negotiations.
• Discussions focused on reducing non-tariff barriers, enhancing cooperation in green technologies, and facilitating services trade.
• Both sides expressed optimism about reaching a mutually beneficial Free Trade Agreement (FTA) by the end of the year.
• Key sectors identified for increased collaboration include renewable energy, digital services, and pharmaceuticals.
• The dialogue also addressed intellectual property rights and sustainable development provisions within the prospective FTA.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: 14th India-EU High-Level Dialogue on Trade and Investment concluded.
Focus on reducing non-tariff barriers, green tech, services trade.
Optimism for FTA by year-end.
Key sectors: renewable energy, digital services, pharmaceuticals.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of an India-EU FTA for India's economic growth and global trade positioning.
Discuss the potential challenges and opportunities in negotiating a comprehensive FTA with the European Union.
Examine how enhanced India-EU cooperation in green technologies can contribute to India's climate goals and industrial development.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of a stronger economic partnership between India and the EU.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and EU flags intertwined, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: The European Union is one of India's largest trading partners, and a comprehensive FTA could unlock significant economic potential for both sides.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Ocean and Climate Monitoring Satellite 'OceanSat-4'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched 'OceanSat-4' aboard a PSLV-XL rocket from Sriharikota.
• OceanSat-4 is designed to provide high-resolution data for ocean color monitoring, sea surface temperature, and atmospheric parameters.
• The satellite will significantly enhance India's capabilities in weather forecasting, climate modeling, and marine resource management.
• It carries advanced sensors including a multi-spectral ocean color monitor and a scatterometer for wind vector measurements.
• This mission underscores India's growing prowess in satellite technology and its commitment to global climate action.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched OceanSat-4 via PSLV-XL.
Purpose: ocean and climate monitoring.
Provides data on ocean color, sea surface temperature, atmospheric parameters.
Enhances weather forecasting, climate modeling, marine resource management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of OceanSat-4 for India's understanding of climate change and its impact on marine ecosystems.
Analyze how advanced ocean monitoring satellites contribute to disaster management and sustainable fishing practices.
Examine India's role in global space cooperation for climate observation and data sharing.
Evaluate the technological advancements in OceanSat-4 compared to its predecessors and other international missions.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of the OceanSat-4 satellite orbiting Earth with oceans visible.
articles.trivia: India's first dedicated ocean remote sensing satellite, OceanSat-1, was launched in 1999.

articles.headline: Economic Survey 2025-26: Rural Consumption and Investment Drive Growth Amidst Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Economic Survey for 2025-26, tabled in Parliament today, highlighted robust growth driven by rural consumption and increased public investment.
• It projected India's GDP growth to remain strong, attributing resilience to agricultural sector performance and government's infrastructure push.
• The survey noted a decline in inflation, crediting proactive monetary policy and supply-side management.
• It also emphasized the need for continued structural reforms to boost manufacturing and create employment opportunities.
• Challenges identified include global economic slowdown, geopolitical uncertainties, and climate-related risks to agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey 2025-26 tabled in Parliament.
Highlights rural consumption and public investment as growth drivers.
Projects strong GDP growth, declining inflation.
Identifies global slowdown, geopolitical risks, climate risks as challenges.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key findings of the Economic Survey 2025-26 regarding India's economic performance and future outlook.
Discuss the role of rural consumption and public investment in sustaining India's growth trajectory.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies in managing inflation and mitigating global economic headwinds.
Suggest further structural reforms needed to enhance manufacturing competitiveness and employment generation in India.
articles.imageDescription: Financial graphs overlaid on an image of rural Indian landscape, symbolizing economic growth.
articles.trivia: The first Economic Survey of India was presented in 1950-51 and was presented along with the Union Budget until 1964, after which it was delinked.

articles.headline: Government Accelerates Implementation of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Invites New Investment Proposals
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced accelerated implementation plans for the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The government is inviting new investment proposals for green hydrogen production and associated infrastructure development.
• Focus is on establishing green hydrogen hubs and promoting indigenous manufacturing of electrolyzers.
• Subsidies and incentives are being streamlined to attract both domestic and international investors.
• This move aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Environmental pollution and degradation.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission implementation accelerated.
New investment proposals invited for production and infrastructure.
Focus on green hydrogen hubs and indigenous electrolyzer manufacturing.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy transition and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and infrastructure in India.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of establishing India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
Examine the role of government policies and private sector investment in achieving the objectives of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized image of a green hydrogen molecule with a leaf, symbolizing clean energy.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources to split water.

articles.headline: Expert Committee Submits Report on Enhanced Conservation Measures for Western Ghats Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• An expert committee, constituted by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, submitted its recommendations for strengthening conservation efforts in the Western Ghats.
• The report proposes stricter regulations on mining, quarrying, and large-scale developmental projects in ecologically sensitive areas.
• It advocates for greater community participation in forest management and sustainable livelihood practices for local communities.
• The committee highlighted the urgent need to address habitat fragmentation and the impact of climate change on biodiversity hotspots.
• Recommendations include establishing a dedicated 'Western Ghats Ecological Authority' with statutory powers.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Climate Change.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Expert committee submitted report on Western Ghats conservation.
Recommends stricter regulations on mining, quarrying, development.
Advocates for community participation and sustainable livelihoods.
Suggested 'Western Ghats Ecological Authority' with statutory powers.
Highlights climate change impact on biodiversity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities and climate change.
Analyze the key recommendations of the expert committee and their potential impact on conservation and development in the region.
Examine the challenges in implementing conservation policies in multi-state ecological zones like the Western Ghats.
Evaluate the importance of community participation and sustainable livelihood models in biodiversity conservation efforts.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forest cover.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity, older than the Himalayas, and home to a vast range of flora and fauna, many endemic.

articles.headline: Law Commission Continues Consultations on Uniform Civil Code; Report Expected Soon
articles.explainer:
• The Law Commission of India announced that it has concluded the final round of consultations with various stakeholders regarding the Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
• A comprehensive report, incorporating diverse viewpoints and legal analyses, is expected to be submitted to the government in the coming weeks.
• Discussions covered the implications of UCC on personal laws, gender justice, and religious freedoms.
• The Commission emphasized the constitutional mandate for a UCC and its potential to promote national integration and equality.
• Public opinion remains divided, with debates focusing on balancing individual rights with community traditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: Law Commission concluded UCC consultations.
Report expected soon to government.
Discussions covered personal laws, gender justice, religious freedoms.
UCC mentioned in Article 44 of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and historical context surrounding the demand for a Uniform Civil Code in India.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of implementing a UCC, particularly concerning gender justice and religious freedom.
Examine the role of the Law Commission in deliberating on sensitive issues like the UCC and building consensus.
Evaluate the socio-political implications of a UCC on India's diverse cultural and religious fabric.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Constitution document with a symbolic image of diverse people, representing unity.
articles.trivia: Goa is the only Indian state currently with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from its Portuguese colonial past.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Outlines Roadmap for 75% Indigenization in Defence Production by 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence unveiled an ambitious roadmap targeting 75% indigenization of defence production by 2030.
• The plan involves boosting domestic manufacturing, encouraging private sector participation, and fostering innovation in defence technology.
• A new 'Positive Indigenisation List' of defence items will be released, prohibiting their import beyond specified timelines.
• Focus areas include advanced weaponry, aerospace components, and naval systems.
• The initiative aims to reduce import dependency, create jobs, and enhance India's strategic autonomy.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: MoD aims for 75% defence indigenization by 2030.
New 'Positive Indigenisation List' to be released.
Focus on domestic manufacturing, private sector, innovation.
Aims to reduce import dependency and create jobs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperative behind India's push for defence indigenization and its implications for national security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for the private sector in contributing to India's defence manufacturing ecosystem.
Evaluate the economic benefits and job creation potential of enhanced domestic defence production.
Examine how indigenization strengthens India's strategic autonomy and its position in global defence markets.
articles.imageDescription: Modern defence equipment (tanks, aircraft) with 'Make in India' logo.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defence equipment, making indigenization a critical policy goal.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Progress and Gaps in Digital Literacy Across Rural India
articles.explainer:
• A new report by NITI Aayog and a leading think tank showcased significant progress in digital literacy rates in rural India over the past five years.
• The report attributed the progress to government initiatives like PMGDISHA and widespread smartphone penetration.
• However, it also highlighted persistent gaps in digital skills, particularly among women and marginalized communities, and in specific remote regions.
• Recommendations include targeted training programs, local language content, and improved internet infrastructure in underserved areas.
• Bridging the digital divide is crucial for inclusive growth and leveraging technology for rural development.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
Human Resource.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog report on digital literacy in rural India.
Shows progress due to PMGDISHA, smartphone penetration.
Gaps persist among women, marginalized groups, remote areas.
Recommends targeted training, local content, better internet infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of digital literacy for inclusive growth and socio-economic empowerment in rural India.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives like PMGDISHA in bridging the rural-urban digital divide.
Examine the specific challenges faced by women and marginalized communities in accessing and utilizing digital resources.
Suggest policy measures and strategies to enhance digital literacy and ensure equitable access to digital services across India.
articles.imageDescription: A rural Indian woman using a smartphone in a village setting.
articles.trivia: The Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA) is one of the world's largest digital literacy programs.

articles.headline: India Hosts Regional Security Dialogue with Central Asian Nations on Counter-Terrorism and Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• India hosted a high-level security dialogue with National Security Advisors (NSAs) from five Central Asian Republics (CARs).
• The discussions primarily focused on regional security challenges, including counter-terrorism efforts and the situation in Afghanistan.
• Connectivity projects, particularly the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC), were also a key agenda item.
• India emphasized its commitment to strengthening strategic partnerships and enhancing security cooperation with the CARs.
• The dialogue concluded with a joint statement reaffirming shared concerns and a resolve for coordinated action against common threats.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India hosted security dialogue with Central Asian Republics (CARs) NSAs.
Focus on counter-terrorism, Afghanistan situation, connectivity (INSTC).
Joint statement on shared concerns and coordinated action.
Aims to strengthen strategic partnerships with CARs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of Central Asia for India's security and economic interests.
Analyze the common security challenges faced by India and Central Asian nations, particularly concerning terrorism and regional instability.
Examine the potential of connectivity projects like INSTC in enhancing India's engagement with the CARs.
Evaluate India's 'Connect Central Asia' policy and its effectiveness in fostering stronger ties.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing India and Central Asian countries connected by trade routes.
articles.trivia: The International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) is a 7,200-km multi-modal network of ship, rail, and road route for moving freight between India, Iran, Azerbaijan, Russia, Central Asia and Europe.

articles.headline: New Archaeological Discovery Sheds Light on Indus Valley Civilization's Maritime Trade
articles.explainer:
• Archaeologists in Gujarat announced the discovery of a new site believed to be an ancient port town linked to the late-Harappan period.
• Excavations revealed unique pottery, weights, and seals indicating extensive maritime trade networks with West Asia.
• The findings provide crucial insights into the economic activities and cultural exchanges of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• This discovery further strengthens the understanding of the advanced urban planning and sophisticated trade systems of the Harappans.
• The site is undergoing further exploration to ascertain its full historical significance and connection to other major Harappan centers.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: New archaeological site discovered in Gujarat, linked to late-Harappan period.
Suggests ancient port town with maritime trade links to West Asia.
Findings include unique pottery, weights, seals.
Provides insights into Indus Valley Civilization's economy and culture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in reshaping our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization's trade networks and cultural interactions.
Analyze the features of Harappan urban planning and economic activities, with a focus on maritime trade.
Examine how archaeological evidence contributes to reconstructing the history of ancient India.
Evaluate the challenges and ethical considerations in archaeological excavations and preservation of historical sites.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's rendition of an ancient Harappan port with ships and trade activity.
articles.trivia: Lothal, also in Gujarat, is one of the most well-known ancient port cities of the Indus Valley Civilization, featuring a sophisticated dockyard.

articles.headline: India's Progress on Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Highlighted, Challenges Remain in Key Areas
articles.explainer:
• A new report by NITI Aayog assessed India's progress across the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for the year 2025.
• Significant strides were noted in poverty reduction (SDG 1), clean energy (SDG 7), and digital infrastructure (SDG 9).
• However, challenges persist in achieving targets related to gender equality (SDG 5), zero hunger (SDG 2), and quality education (SDG 4).
• The report emphasized the need for accelerated efforts, especially at the sub-national level, and enhanced data collection for effective monitoring.
• It called for greater inter-ministerial coordination and public-private partnerships to bridge the remaining gaps by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog report assessed India's SDG progress for 2025.
Progress in SDG 1 (Poverty), SDG 7 (Clean Energy), SDG 9 (Infrastructure).
Challenges in SDG 2 (Zero Hunger), SDG 4 (Education), SDG 5 (Gender Equality).
Calls for accelerated efforts, better data, inter-ministerial coordination.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's performance on the Sustainable Development Goals, highlighting areas of progress and persistent challenges.
Analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to the gaps in achieving SDGs related to gender equality, hunger, and education.
Examine the role of NITI Aayog and sub-national governments in monitoring and accelerating SDG implementation.
Suggest innovative strategies and policy interventions to ensure India achieves its SDG targets by 2030.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic showing the 17 Sustainable Development Goals icons.
articles.trivia: The 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, adopted by all United Nations Member States in 2015, provides a shared blueprint for peace and prosperity for people and the planet.

articles.headline: Global Supply Chain Disruptions Continue to Impact Indian Manufacturing Sector
articles.explainer:
• A recent industry report indicated that global supply chain disruptions, exacerbated by geopolitical tensions, continue to affect India's manufacturing sector.
• Sectors like electronics, automotive, and pharmaceuticals are experiencing delays in raw material procurement and increased logistics costs.
• The report emphasized the need for greater diversification of supply sources and strengthening domestic production capabilities.
• Government is exploring new trade agreements and incentives to mitigate the impact and build resilient supply chains.
• This ongoing challenge highlights vulnerabilities in globalized production systems and the push towards 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Global supply chain disruptions impacting Indian manufacturing.
Electronics, auto, pharma sectors face delays, higher costs.
Need for supply diversification and domestic production.
Government exploring new trade agreements.
Highlights 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' relevance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted impact of global supply chain disruptions on India's manufacturing sector and overall economy.
Analyze the strategies being adopted by the Indian government and industry to build more resilient and diversified supply chains.
Examine the role of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in mitigating vulnerabilities exposed by global supply chain shocks.
Evaluate the potential for India to emerge as an alternative manufacturing hub amidst evolving global trade dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: Interconnected global map with factory icons and broken chain links, symbolizing disruptions.
articles.trivia: The COVID-19 pandemic and subsequent geopolitical events significantly highlighted the fragility of global 'just-in-time' supply chains.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Scheme for Skill Development in AI, Robotics, and Data Science
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship unveiled a new national scheme aimed at upskilling and reskilling the workforce in emerging technologies.
• The program focuses on Artificial Intelligence (AI), Robotics, Data Science, and Blockchain, aligning with industry demands.
• It will leverage public-private partnerships to establish state-of-the-art training centers and offer certifications recognized globally.
• Special emphasis is placed on youth and women, ensuring equitable access to future-ready skills.
• The initiative is crucial for enhancing India's competitiveness in the global digital economy and addressing the future of work.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: New skill development scheme launched by MSDE.
Focus on AI, Robotics, Data Science, Blockchain.
Leverages public-private partnerships for training centers.
Emphasizes youth and women for future-ready skills.
mainsPoints: Discuss the imperative of skill development in emerging technologies for India's demographic dividend and economic growth.
Analyze how the new scheme addresses the challenges of rapid technological change and the future of work.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in creating a robust ecosystem for advanced skill training.
Evaluate the potential impact of this initiative on youth employment and women's participation in the digital economy.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern lab learning about AI and robotics.
articles.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations in the world, making skill development crucial for harnessing its demographic dividend.

articles.headline: Air Quality Index in Major Indian Cities Shows Mixed Trends; Winter Pollution Remains a Concern
articles.explainer:
• A report by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) indicated mixed trends in the Air Quality Index (AQI) across major Indian cities.
• While some cities showed slight improvement due to localized measures, others, particularly in the Indo-Gangetic Plain, continue to battle 'poor' to 'very poor' air quality.
• Winter pollution factors, including stubble burning, vehicular emissions, and industrial activities, remain dominant contributors.
• The report called for stricter enforcement of emission norms, promotion of public transport, and adoption of cleaner energy sources.
• Public health advisories were reissued, urging vulnerable groups to take precautions against respiratory illnesses.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: CPCB report on AQI in major Indian cities shows mixed trends.
Indo-Gangetic Plain cities face 'poor' to 'very poor' air quality.
Winter pollution factors: stubble burning, vehicular emissions, industry.
Calls for stricter emission norms, public transport, cleaner energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major causes and health impacts of air pollution in Indian cities, particularly during winter months.
Analyze the effectiveness of existing government policies and measures in controlling air pollution.
Examine the challenges in implementing comprehensive air quality management strategies across states and sectors.
Suggest innovative solutions and citizen-centric approaches to mitigate air pollution and improve public health.
articles.imageDescription: Smoggy cityscape with an AQI monitor showing 'Very Poor' reading.
articles.trivia: The Air Quality Index (AQI) is a tool for effectively communicating air quality status to people in terms that are easy to understand. It has six categories of air quality.

articles.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Talks on Indus Water Treaty; Focus on Hydroelectric Projects
articles.explainer:
• Officials from India and Pakistan held technical discussions under the framework of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) in New Delhi.
• The talks focused on resolving disputes related to the design and operation of hydroelectric power projects being constructed by India on the Chenab river.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, a crucial water-sharing agreement.
• While no major breakthrough was announced, the dialogue was seen as constructive, fostering communication between the two nations.
• The Permanent Indus Commission is expected to meet again later this year to continue discussions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India-Pakistan technical talks on Indus Water Treaty (IWT) held.
Discussions on Indian hydroelectric projects on Chenab river.
Both sides committed to IWT provisions.
Permanent Indus Commission to meet again.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical significance and key provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) between India and Pakistan.
Analyze the challenges and complexities in managing water resources and hydroelectric projects under the IWT framework.
Examine the role of water diplomacy in fostering cooperation and mitigating tensions between riparian states.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of water-sharing agreements in South Asia.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system flowing through India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty was signed in 1960 and is one of the most successful water-sharing agreements in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Review Meeting Held on Implementation Progress of National Education Policy 2020
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Discussions focused on the transformation in school education, higher education reforms, and vocational training integration.
• While significant strides were noted in curriculum redesign and teacher training, challenges remain in infrastructure development and digital learning access.
• The Minister emphasized the need for accelerated efforts to achieve the policy's transformative goals by the target timelines.
• Stakeholders, including state education departments and universities, were urged to enhance collaboration for effective implementation.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Review meeting on National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation.
Progress in curriculum, teacher training noted.
Challenges in infrastructure, digital learning access.
Minister urged accelerated efforts and stakeholder collaboration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key objectives and transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 for India's education system.
Analyze the progress made and the persistent challenges in implementing NEP 2020 across different levels of education.
Examine the role of digital learning and vocational training in achieving the goals of the NEP.
Suggest strategies for effective collaboration between central and state governments, and other stakeholders, to ensure successful implementation of education reforms.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing updated education system.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 policy, making it the first new education policy in 34 years.

articles.headline: India Proposes New G20 Initiative for Enhanced Climate Finance Mobilization for Developing Nations
articles.explainer:
• India, as part of its ongoing G20 engagement, proposed a new initiative aimed at significantly increasing climate finance mobilization for developing countries.
• The proposal emphasizes innovative financing mechanisms, including green bonds and blended finance, to meet the $100 billion annual climate finance goal.
• It also calls for greater transparency and accountability in climate finance flows from developed to developing nations.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the funding gap for climate adaptation and mitigation projects in vulnerable economies.
• India urged G20 members to adopt a time-bound roadmap for achieving the climate finance commitments.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India proposed new G20 initiative for climate finance mobilization.
Focus on innovative mechanisms: green bonds, blended finance.
Calls for transparency and accountability in climate finance.
Aims to bridge funding gap for adaptation/mitigation in developing nations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical importance of climate finance for developing countries in addressing climate change challenges.
Analyze India's role and proposals within the G20 framework for enhancing global climate finance mobilization.
Examine the efficacy of innovative financing mechanisms like green bonds and blended finance in achieving climate goals.
Evaluate the challenges faced by developing nations in accessing adequate and timely climate finance and potential solutions.
articles.imageDescription: Globe with currency symbols and green leaves, symbolizing climate finance.
articles.trivia: The commitment by developed countries to jointly mobilize $100 billion per year by 2020 for climate action in developing countries was first made at COP15 in Copenhagen in 2009.

highlights: Centre launches National Urban Climate Resilience Mission 2.0, focusing on water security and green infrastructure in cities.
Supreme Court upholds Digital Personal Data Protection Act, emphasizing fundamental right to privacy and data protection compliance.
India-EU High-Level Trade Dialogue progresses, signaling potential for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) by year-end.
ISRO successfully launches 'OceanSat-4' for advanced ocean and climate monitoring, enhancing India's climate modeling capabilities.
Economic Survey 2025-26 highlights rural consumption and public investment as key drivers of India's robust economic growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Urban Climate Resilience Mission (NUCRM) 2.0, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to enhance urban infrastructure resilience against climate change impacts.
2. It primarily focuses on promoting public transport systems and electric vehicles.
3. The mission encourages public-private partnerships and nature-based solutions.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NUCRM 2.0 explicitly aims to enhance urban infrastructure resilience against climate change impacts. Statement 2 is incorrect: While public transport and EVs are climate-friendly, the primary focus areas mentioned for NUCRM 2.0 are water security, waste management, and green infrastructure. Statement 3 is correct: The mission emphasizes public-private partnerships and the adoption of nature-based solutions.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is mandated to be established by the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA) for its effective implementation, as highlighted by the Supreme Court?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's ruling, while upholding the DPDPA, specifically directed the government to expedite the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India, which is the regulatory body envisioned by the Act.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'OceanSat-4' satellite recently launched by ISRO:
1. It is designed for high-resolution data on ocean color monitoring and sea surface temperature.
2. It was launched using the Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV).
3. The satellite will aid in weather forecasting and marine resource management.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: OceanSat-4 provides data for ocean color monitoring and sea surface temperature. Statement 2 is incorrect: It was launched aboard a PSLV-XL rocket, not GSLV. Statement 3 is correct: The satellite will enhance India's capabilities in weather forecasting, climate modeling, and marine resource management.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Positive Indigenisation List', recently seen in news, is associated with which of the following sectors in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Ministry of Defence announced a roadmap for 75% indigenization in defence production by 2030, which includes releasing a new 'Positive Indigenisation List' of defence items whose import will be prohibited beyond specified timelines.

prelimsMocks.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution contains the provision for a Uniform Civil Code (UCC)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Article 44 of the Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution states: 'The State shall endeavour to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.'

mainsMocks.question: Evaluate the significance of the National Urban Climate Resilience Mission 2.0 in addressing the multifaceted challenges of urban climate change and sustainable development in India. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Disaster and disaster management.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NUCRM 2.0 and its core objectives. Discuss how it tackles challenges like water scarcity, waste management, and infrastructure vulnerability. Highlight its emphasis on climate-responsive planning, green infrastructure, and community participation. Conclude on its role in achieving India's climate goals and SDG 11.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's upholding of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA) marks a critical juncture for digital rights in India. Analyze the implications of this judgment for individual privacy, corporate accountability, and India's position in global data governance. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Indian Constitution—Fundamental Rights.
mainsMocks.approach: Start with the context of the SC judgment and DPDPA's importance. For individual privacy, discuss the strengthened right to privacy and control over personal data. For corporate accountability, elaborate on compliance mandates for data fiduciaries, cross-border data transfer rules, and the role of the Data Protection Board. For global data governance, discuss how this positions India alongside nations with robust data protection laws and its influence on international digital norms. Conclude on the balance between innovation and privacy.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant progress, India continues to face challenges in achieving several Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly in areas like gender equality, hunger, and quality education. Discuss the underlying socio-economic factors contributing to these gaps and suggest comprehensive strategies to accelerate SDG attainment by 2030. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections; Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly acknowledge India's overall SDG progress. Then, delve into specific SDGs (2, 4, 5) and identify socio-economic factors: poverty, patriarchal norms, access disparities, digital divide, lack of infrastructure, funding gaps. Suggest strategies: targeted interventions, inter-ministerial coordination, community engagement, technology leverage, public-private partnerships, enhanced data monitoring, and investment in human capital. Emphasize localized approaches and equitable resource distribution.

quickBytes.text: The National Urban Climate Resilience Mission 2.0 focuses on water security, waste management, and green infrastructure.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA) ensures the fundamental right to privacy in the digital realm.
India and the EU are optimistic about finalizing a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) by the end of 2026.
ISRO's 'OceanSat-4' satellite will provide high-resolution data for ocean color monitoring and sea surface temperature.
The Economic Survey 2025-26 identified rural consumption and public investment as key drivers of India's growth.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
Article 44 of the Indian Constitution is a Directive Principle of State Policy concerning the Uniform Civil Code.
India aims for 75% indigenization in defence production by 2030 to enhance strategic autonomy.
The Indus Water Treaty (1960) between India and Pakistan regulates the sharing of Indus river system waters.

GS1.headline: New Archaeological Discovery Sheds Light on Indus Valley Civilization's Maritime Trade
GS1.explainer:
• Archaeologists in Gujarat announced the discovery of a new site believed to be an ancient port town linked to the late-Harappan period.
• Excavations revealed unique pottery, weights, and seals indicating extensive maritime trade networks with West Asia.
• The findings provide crucial insights into the economic activities and cultural exchanges of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• This discovery further strengthens the understanding of the advanced urban planning and sophisticated trade systems of the Harappans.
• The site is undergoing further exploration to ascertain its full historical significance and connection to other major Harappan centers.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: New archaeological site discovered in Gujarat, linked to late-Harappan period.
Suggests ancient port town with maritime trade links to West Asia.
Findings include unique pottery, weights, seals.
Provides insights into Indus Valley Civilization's economy and culture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in reshaping our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization's trade networks and cultural interactions.
Analyze the features of Harappan urban planning and economic activities, with a focus on maritime trade.
Examine how archaeological evidence contributes to reconstructing the history of ancient India.
Evaluate the challenges and ethical considerations in archaeological excavations and preservation of historical sites.
GS1.imageDescription: Artist's rendition of an ancient Harappan port with ships and trade activity.
GS1.trivia: Lothal, also in Gujarat, is one of the most well-known ancient port cities of the Indus Valley Civilization, featuring a sophisticated dockyard.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Robust Digital Privacy Framework, Stresses Data Protection Compliance
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today delivered a landmark judgment, upholding the core tenets of the recently enacted Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA).
• The ruling emphasized the fundamental right to privacy in the digital age, mandating stringent compliance for data fiduciaries and processors.
• It clarified certain ambiguities regarding cross-border data transfers and the 'significant data fiduciary' classification.
• The court also directed the government to expedite the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India.
• This judgment is expected to significantly impact how technology companies operate and handle user data in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA).
Emphasized fundamental right to privacy.
Clarified cross-border data transfer rules.
Directed establishment of Data Protection Board of India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on digital privacy for individuals and corporations in India.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities presented by the DPDPA in fostering a secure digital economy.
Examine the balance between national security concerns and individual privacy rights in the context of digital data protection.
Evaluate India's position in global data governance with the enforcement of its robust data protection law.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel resting on a legal document with a digital lock icon, symbolizing data protection.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, paving the way for data protection legislation.

GS2.headline: India-EU High-Level Trade Dialogue Sees Significant Progress on Key Sectors
GS2.explainer:
• The 14th round of the India-EU High-Level Dialogue on Trade and Investment concluded with positive outcomes, signaling potential breakthroughs in FTA negotiations.
• Discussions focused on reducing non-tariff barriers, enhancing cooperation in green technologies, and facilitating services trade.
• Both sides expressed optimism about reaching a mutually beneficial Free Trade Agreement (FTA) by the end of the year.
• Key sectors identified for increased collaboration include renewable energy, digital services, and pharmaceuticals.
• The dialogue also addressed intellectual property rights and sustainable development provisions within the prospective FTA.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: 14th India-EU High-Level Dialogue on Trade and Investment concluded.
Focus on reducing non-tariff barriers, green tech, services trade.
Optimism for FTA by year-end.
Key sectors: renewable energy, digital services, pharmaceuticals.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of an India-EU FTA for India's economic growth and global trade positioning.
Discuss the potential challenges and opportunities in negotiating a comprehensive FTA with the European Union.
Examine how enhanced India-EU cooperation in green technologies can contribute to India's climate goals and industrial development.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of a stronger economic partnership between India and the EU.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and EU flags intertwined, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The European Union is one of India's largest trading partners, and a comprehensive FTA could unlock significant economic potential for both sides.

GS2.headline: Law Commission Continues Consultations on Uniform Civil Code; Report Expected Soon
GS2.explainer:
• The Law Commission of India announced that it has concluded the final round of consultations with various stakeholders regarding the Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
• A comprehensive report, incorporating diverse viewpoints and legal analyses, is expected to be submitted to the government in the coming weeks.
• Discussions covered the implications of UCC on personal laws, gender justice, and religious freedoms.
• The Commission emphasized the constitutional mandate for a UCC and its potential to promote national integration and equality.
• Public opinion remains divided, with debates focusing on balancing individual rights with community traditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: Law Commission concluded UCC consultations.
Report expected soon to government.
Discussions covered personal laws, gender justice, religious freedoms.
UCC mentioned in Article 44 of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and historical context surrounding the demand for a Uniform Civil Code in India.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of implementing a UCC, particularly concerning gender justice and religious freedom.
Examine the role of the Law Commission in deliberating on sensitive issues like the UCC and building consensus.
Evaluate the socio-political implications of a UCC on India's diverse cultural and religious fabric.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian Constitution document with a symbolic image of diverse people, representing unity.
GS2.trivia: Goa is the only Indian state currently with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from its Portuguese colonial past.

GS2.headline: Report Highlights Progress and Gaps in Digital Literacy Across Rural India
GS2.explainer:
• A new report by NITI Aayog and a leading think tank showcased significant progress in digital literacy rates in rural India over the past five years.
• The report attributed the progress to government initiatives like PMGDISHA and widespread smartphone penetration.
• However, it also highlighted persistent gaps in digital skills, particularly among women and marginalized communities, and in specific remote regions.
• Recommendations include targeted training programs, local language content, and improved internet infrastructure in underserved areas.
• Bridging the digital divide is crucial for inclusive growth and leveraging technology for rural development.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
Human Resource.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog report on digital literacy in rural India.
Shows progress due to PMGDISHA, smartphone penetration.
Gaps persist among women, marginalized groups, remote areas.
Recommends targeted training, local content, better internet infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of digital literacy for inclusive growth and socio-economic empowerment in rural India.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives like PMGDISHA in bridging the rural-urban digital divide.
Examine the specific challenges faced by women and marginalized communities in accessing and utilizing digital resources.
Suggest policy measures and strategies to enhance digital literacy and ensure equitable access to digital services across India.
GS2.imageDescription: A rural Indian woman using a smartphone in a village setting.
GS2.trivia: The Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA) is one of the world's largest digital literacy programs.

GS2.headline: India Hosts Regional Security Dialogue with Central Asian Nations on Counter-Terrorism and Connectivity
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted a high-level security dialogue with National Security Advisors (NSAs) from five Central Asian Republics (CARs).
• The discussions primarily focused on regional security challenges, including counter-terrorism efforts and the situation in Afghanistan.
• Connectivity projects, particularly the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC), were also a key agenda item.
• India emphasized its commitment to strengthening strategic partnerships and enhancing security cooperation with the CARs.
• The dialogue concluded with a joint statement reaffirming shared concerns and a resolve for coordinated action against common threats.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India hosted security dialogue with Central Asian Republics (CARs) NSAs.
Focus on counter-terrorism, Afghanistan situation, connectivity (INSTC).
Joint statement on shared concerns and coordinated action.
Aims to strengthen strategic partnerships with CARs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of Central Asia for India's security and economic interests.
Analyze the common security challenges faced by India and Central Asian nations, particularly concerning terrorism and regional instability.
Examine the potential of connectivity projects like INSTC in enhancing India's engagement with the CARs.
Evaluate India's 'Connect Central Asia' policy and its effectiveness in fostering stronger ties.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing India and Central Asian countries connected by trade routes.
GS2.trivia: The International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) is a 7,200-km multi-modal network of ship, rail, and road route for moving freight between India, Iran, Azerbaijan, Russia, Central Asia and Europe.

GS2.headline: India's Progress on Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Highlighted, Challenges Remain in Key Areas
GS2.explainer:
• A new report by NITI Aayog assessed India's progress across the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for the year 2025.
• Significant strides were noted in poverty reduction (SDG 1), clean energy (SDG 7), and digital infrastructure (SDG 9).
• However, challenges persist in achieving targets related to gender equality (SDG 5), zero hunger (SDG 2), and quality education (SDG 4).
• The report emphasized the need for accelerated efforts, especially at the sub-national level, and enhanced data collection for effective monitoring.
• It called for greater inter-ministerial coordination and public-private partnerships to bridge the remaining gaps by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog report assessed India's SDG progress for 2025.
Progress in SDG 1 (Poverty), SDG 7 (Clean Energy), SDG 9 (Infrastructure).
Challenges in SDG 2 (Zero Hunger), SDG 4 (Education), SDG 5 (Gender Equality).
Calls for accelerated efforts, better data, inter-ministerial coordination.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's performance on the Sustainable Development Goals, highlighting areas of progress and persistent challenges.
Analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to the gaps in achieving SDGs related to gender equality, hunger, and education.
Examine the role of NITI Aayog and sub-national governments in monitoring and accelerating SDG implementation.
Suggest innovative strategies and policy interventions to ensure India achieves its SDG targets by 2030.
GS2.imageDescription: Infographic showing the 17 Sustainable Development Goals icons.
GS2.trivia: The 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, adopted by all United Nations Member States in 2015, provides a shared blueprint for peace and prosperity for people and the planet.

GS2.headline: Government Launches New Scheme for Skill Development in AI, Robotics, and Data Science
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship unveiled a new national scheme aimed at upskilling and reskilling the workforce in emerging technologies.
• The program focuses on Artificial Intelligence (AI), Robotics, Data Science, and Blockchain, aligning with industry demands.
• It will leverage public-private partnerships to establish state-of-the-art training centers and offer certifications recognized globally.
• Special emphasis is placed on youth and women, ensuring equitable access to future-ready skills.
• The initiative is crucial for enhancing India's competitiveness in the global digital economy and addressing the future of work.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: New skill development scheme launched by MSDE.
Focus on AI, Robotics, Data Science, Blockchain.
Leverages public-private partnerships for training centers.
Emphasizes youth and women for future-ready skills.
mainsPoints: Discuss the imperative of skill development in emerging technologies for India's demographic dividend and economic growth.
Analyze how the new scheme addresses the challenges of rapid technological change and the future of work.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in creating a robust ecosystem for advanced skill training.
Evaluate the potential impact of this initiative on youth employment and women's participation in the digital economy.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern lab learning about AI and robotics.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations in the world, making skill development crucial for harnessing its demographic dividend.

GS2.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Talks on Indus Water Treaty; Focus on Hydroelectric Projects
GS2.explainer:
• Officials from India and Pakistan held technical discussions under the framework of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) in New Delhi.
• The talks focused on resolving disputes related to the design and operation of hydroelectric power projects being constructed by India on the Chenab river.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, a crucial water-sharing agreement.
• While no major breakthrough was announced, the dialogue was seen as constructive, fostering communication between the two nations.
• The Permanent Indus Commission is expected to meet again later this year to continue discussions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India-Pakistan technical talks on Indus Water Treaty (IWT) held.
Discussions on Indian hydroelectric projects on Chenab river.
Both sides committed to IWT provisions.
Permanent Indus Commission to meet again.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical significance and key provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) between India and Pakistan.
Analyze the challenges and complexities in managing water resources and hydroelectric projects under the IWT framework.
Examine the role of water diplomacy in fostering cooperation and mitigating tensions between riparian states.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of water-sharing agreements in South Asia.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system flowing through India and Pakistan.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty was signed in 1960 and is one of the most successful water-sharing agreements in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: Review Meeting Held on Implementation Progress of National Education Policy 2020
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Discussions focused on the transformation in school education, higher education reforms, and vocational training integration.
• While significant strides were noted in curriculum redesign and teacher training, challenges remain in infrastructure development and digital learning access.
• The Minister emphasized the need for accelerated efforts to achieve the policy's transformative goals by the target timelines.
• Stakeholders, including state education departments and universities, were urged to enhance collaboration for effective implementation.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Review meeting on National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation.
Progress in curriculum, teacher training noted.
Challenges in infrastructure, digital learning access.
Minister urged accelerated efforts and stakeholder collaboration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key objectives and transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 for India's education system.
Analyze the progress made and the persistent challenges in implementing NEP 2020 across different levels of education.
Examine the role of digital learning and vocational training in achieving the goals of the NEP.
Suggest strategies for effective collaboration between central and state governments, and other stakeholders, to ensure successful implementation of education reforms.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing updated education system.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 policy, making it the first new education policy in 34 years.

GS2.headline: India Proposes New G20 Initiative for Enhanced Climate Finance Mobilization for Developing Nations
GS2.explainer:
• India, as part of its ongoing G20 engagement, proposed a new initiative aimed at significantly increasing climate finance mobilization for developing countries.
• The proposal emphasizes innovative financing mechanisms, including green bonds and blended finance, to meet the $100 billion annual climate finance goal.
• It also calls for greater transparency and accountability in climate finance flows from developed to developing nations.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the funding gap for climate adaptation and mitigation projects in vulnerable economies.
• India urged G20 members to adopt a time-bound roadmap for achieving the climate finance commitments.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India proposed new G20 initiative for climate finance mobilization.
Focus on innovative mechanisms: green bonds, blended finance.
Calls for transparency and accountability in climate finance.
Aims to bridge funding gap for adaptation/mitigation in developing nations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical importance of climate finance for developing countries in addressing climate change challenges.
Analyze India's role and proposals within the G20 framework for enhancing global climate finance mobilization.
Examine the efficacy of innovative financing mechanisms like green bonds and blended finance in achieving climate goals.
Evaluate the challenges faced by developing nations in accessing adequate and timely climate finance and potential solutions.
GS2.imageDescription: Globe with currency symbols and green leaves, symbolizing climate finance.
GS2.trivia: The commitment by developed countries to jointly mobilize $100 billion per year by 2020 for climate action in developing countries was first made at COP15 in Copenhagen in 2009.

GS3.headline: Centre Launches National Urban Climate Resilience Mission 2.0 to Bolster City Preparedness
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet on Monday approved the launch of the National Urban Climate Resilience Mission (NUCRM) 2.0, a significant upgrade to the existing framework.
• The mission aims to enhance urban infrastructure resilience against climate change impacts, focusing on water security, waste management, and green infrastructure.
• It will promote climate-responsive urban planning, early warning systems, and capacity building for municipal bodies and local communities.
• A substantial allocation has been made for integrated urban water management projects, including rainwater harvesting and wastewater recycling plants.
• The mission emphasizes public-private partnerships and the adoption of nature-based solutions for sustainable urban development.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: NUCRM 2.0 launched by Union Cabinet.
Focus areas: water security, waste management, green infrastructure.
Aims to enhance urban infrastructure resilience.
Promotes public-private partnerships and nature-based solutions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of NUCRM 2.0 in achieving India's climate commitments and SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities).
Analyze the challenges in urban climate resilience and how NUCRM 2.0 proposes to address them.
Evaluate the role of integrated urban water management and green infrastructure in mitigating climate risks in Indian cities.
Examine the potential for public-private partnerships and community involvement in the success of such missions.
GS3.imageDescription: Conceptual image of a green, resilient smart city with sustainable infrastructure.
GS3.trivia: Globally, over half of humanity lives in urban areas, and this proportion is expected to reach 68% by 2050, making urban resilience critical for future sustainability.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Ocean and Climate Monitoring Satellite 'OceanSat-4'
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched 'OceanSat-4' aboard a PSLV-XL rocket from Sriharikota.
• OceanSat-4 is designed to provide high-resolution data for ocean color monitoring, sea surface temperature, and atmospheric parameters.
• The satellite will significantly enhance India's capabilities in weather forecasting, climate modeling, and marine resource management.
• It carries advanced sensors including a multi-spectral ocean color monitor and a scatterometer for wind vector measurements.
• This mission underscores India's growing prowess in satellite technology and its commitment to global climate action.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched OceanSat-4 via PSLV-XL.
Purpose: ocean and climate monitoring.
Provides data on ocean color, sea surface temperature, atmospheric parameters.
Enhances weather forecasting, climate modeling, marine resource management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of OceanSat-4 for India's understanding of climate change and its impact on marine ecosystems.
Analyze how advanced ocean monitoring satellites contribute to disaster management and sustainable fishing practices.
Examine India's role in global space cooperation for climate observation and data sharing.
Evaluate the technological advancements in OceanSat-4 compared to its predecessors and other international missions.
GS3.imageDescription: Artist's impression of the OceanSat-4 satellite orbiting Earth with oceans visible.
GS3.trivia: India's first dedicated ocean remote sensing satellite, OceanSat-1, was launched in 1999.

GS3.headline: Economic Survey 2025-26: Rural Consumption and Investment Drive Growth Amidst Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Economic Survey for 2025-26, tabled in Parliament today, highlighted robust growth driven by rural consumption and increased public investment.
• It projected India's GDP growth to remain strong, attributing resilience to agricultural sector performance and government's infrastructure push.
• The survey noted a decline in inflation, crediting proactive monetary policy and supply-side management.
• It also emphasized the need for continued structural reforms to boost manufacturing and create employment opportunities.
• Challenges identified include global economic slowdown, geopolitical uncertainties, and climate-related risks to agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey 2025-26 tabled in Parliament.
Highlights rural consumption and public investment as growth drivers.
Projects strong GDP growth, declining inflation.
Identifies global slowdown, geopolitical risks, climate risks as challenges.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key findings of the Economic Survey 2025-26 regarding India's economic performance and future outlook.
Discuss the role of rural consumption and public investment in sustaining India's growth trajectory.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies in managing inflation and mitigating global economic headwinds.
Suggest further structural reforms needed to enhance manufacturing competitiveness and employment generation in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Financial graphs overlaid on an image of rural Indian landscape, symbolizing economic growth.
GS3.trivia: The first Economic Survey of India was presented in 1950-51 and was presented along with the Union Budget until 1964, after which it was delinked.

GS3.headline: Government Accelerates Implementation of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Invites New Investment Proposals
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced accelerated implementation plans for the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The government is inviting new investment proposals for green hydrogen production and associated infrastructure development.
• Focus is on establishing green hydrogen hubs and promoting indigenous manufacturing of electrolyzers.
• Subsidies and incentives are being streamlined to attract both domestic and international investors.
• This move aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Environmental pollution and degradation.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission implementation accelerated.
New investment proposals invited for production and infrastructure.
Focus on green hydrogen hubs and indigenous electrolyzer manufacturing.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy transition and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and infrastructure in India.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of establishing India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
Examine the role of government policies and private sector investment in achieving the objectives of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
GS3.imageDescription: Stylized image of a green hydrogen molecule with a leaf, symbolizing clean energy.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources to split water.

GS3.headline: Expert Committee Submits Report on Enhanced Conservation Measures for Western Ghats Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• An expert committee, constituted by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, submitted its recommendations for strengthening conservation efforts in the Western Ghats.
• The report proposes stricter regulations on mining, quarrying, and large-scale developmental projects in ecologically sensitive areas.
• It advocates for greater community participation in forest management and sustainable livelihood practices for local communities.
• The committee highlighted the urgent need to address habitat fragmentation and the impact of climate change on biodiversity hotspots.
• Recommendations include establishing a dedicated 'Western Ghats Ecological Authority' with statutory powers.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Climate Change.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Expert committee submitted report on Western Ghats conservation.
Recommends stricter regulations on mining, quarrying, development.
Advocates for community participation and sustainable livelihoods.
Suggested 'Western Ghats Ecological Authority' with statutory powers.
Highlights climate change impact on biodiversity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities and climate change.
Analyze the key recommendations of the expert committee and their potential impact on conservation and development in the region.
Examine the challenges in implementing conservation policies in multi-state ecological zones like the Western Ghats.
Evaluate the importance of community participation and sustainable livelihood models in biodiversity conservation efforts.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forest cover.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity, older than the Himalayas, and home to a vast range of flora and fauna, many endemic.

GS3.headline: Defence Ministry Outlines Roadmap for 75% Indigenization in Defence Production by 2030
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence unveiled an ambitious roadmap targeting 75% indigenization of defence production by 2030.
• The plan involves boosting domestic manufacturing, encouraging private sector participation, and fostering innovation in defence technology.
• A new 'Positive Indigenisation List' of defence items will be released, prohibiting their import beyond specified timelines.
• Focus areas include advanced weaponry, aerospace components, and naval systems.
• The initiative aims to reduce import dependency, create jobs, and enhance India's strategic autonomy.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: MoD aims for 75% defence indigenization by 2030.
New 'Positive Indigenisation List' to be released.
Focus on domestic manufacturing, private sector, innovation.
Aims to reduce import dependency and create jobs.
